@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s plan was flawed: in call_2QhAFC652Ro2UVKplVez72ZL it defined “most frequently copied” as COUNT(DISTINCT f.repo_name) over files.id (and then in call_3NawLFMOz3E4Yxsgw9aUawKh listed all repos containing that id), rather than using the dataset’s copy metadata (e.g., from repo_data_description) to identify the top-copied non-binary Swift file and the single Swift-language repository, leading to the wrong repository (and multiple repos) instead of the ground-truth SwiftAndroid/swift.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

The agent limited its search to the first 10,000 contents rows (call_UpL3MbiJCKJtLwGqt9WjyYHN) and then regex-parsed repo_data_description to infer copy counts (call_hIlkSt0w17NFomu1OILFXnym) without joining to languages or counting across the full files table, adding an unrequested constraint and missing the true top file/repo (SwiftAndroid/swift).

The agent ordered and limited candidates by COUNT(*) of rows (which was ~1 per id) in call_zyBa8RDtW3ddvCAcDjzNMBIA, then parsed only those 50 descriptions in call_dv6aCDzgA3GeNNFJAfOaYmzM to pick a maximum, so it never searched the full dataset for the true most-copied non-binary Swift file and reported the wrong repository.

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
By filtering non-binary files with NOT LIKE '%binary%' on the natural-language repo_data_description (call_a3POUD4chPzV8QsUj2015vZD) after a LEFT JOIN, the agent effectively excluded valid non-binary Swift files (whose descriptions still include the word "binary") and counted only rows lacking contents metadata, leading it to select id a6fb31... and arbitrarily return othierry/future instead of the true top repository SwiftAndroid/swift.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
